verses.latin_vulgate: Quæ postquam gesta sunt, tentavit Deus Abraham, et dixit ad eum : Abraham, Abraham. At ille respondit : Adsum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיְהִ֗י אַחַר֙ הַדְּבָרִ֣ים הָאֵ֔לֶּה וְהָ֣אֱלֹהִ֔ים נִסָּ֖ה אֶת־אַבְרָהָ֑ם וַיֹּ֣אמֶר אֵלָ֔יו אַבְרָהָ֖ם וַיֹּ֥אמֶר הִנֵּֽנִי׃
verses.douay_rheims: After these things, God tempted Abraham, and said to him: Abraham, Abraham. And he answered: Here I am.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja koska se oli tapahtunut, koetteli Jumala Abrahamia, ja sanoi hänelle: Abraham. Ja hän vastasi: Katso, tässä minä olen.
verses.king_james: And it came to pass after these things, that God did tempt Abraham, and said unto him, Abraham: and he said, Behold, here I am.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ait illi : Tolle filium tuum unigenitum, quem diligis, Isaac, et vade in terram visionis, atque ibi offeres eum in holocaustum super unum montium quem monstravero tibi.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֹּ֡אמֶר קַח־נָ֠א אֶת־בִּנְךָ֨ אֶת־יְחִֽידְךָ֤ אֲשֶׁר־אָהַ֙בְתָּ֙ אֶת־יִצְחָ֔ק וְלֶ֨ךְ־לְךָ֔ אֶל־אֶ֖רֶץ הַמֹּרִיָּ֑ה וְהַעֲלֵ֤הוּ שָׁם֙ לְעֹלָ֔ה עַ֚ל אַחַ֣ד הֶֽהָרִ֔ים אֲשֶׁ֖ר אֹמַ֥ר אֵלֶֽיךָ׃
verses.douay_rheims: He said to him: Take thy only begotten son Isaac, whom thou lovest, and go into the land of vision; and there thou shalt offer him for an holocaust upon one of the mountains which I will shew thee.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja hän sanoi: ota nyt Isaak sinun ainoa poikas, jotas rakastat, ja mene Morian maalle; ja uhraa häntä siellä polttouhriksi yhdellä niistä vuorista, jonka minä sinulle sanova olen.
verses.king_james: And he said, Take now thy son, thine only son Isaac, whom thou lovest, and get thee into the land of Moriah; and offer him there for a burnt offering upon one of the mountains which I will tell thee of.

verses.latin_vulgate: Igitur Abraham de nocte consurgens, stravit asinum suum, ducens secum duos juvenes, et Isaac filium suum : cumque concidisset ligna in holocaustum, abiit ad locum quem præceperat ei Deus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיַּשְׁכֵּ֨ם אַבְרָהָ֜ם בַּבֹּ֗קֶר וַֽיַּחֲבֹשׁ֙ אֶת־חֲמֹר֔וֹ וַיִּקַּ֞ח אֶת־שְׁנֵ֤י נְעָרָיו֙ אִתּ֔וֹ וְאֵ֖ת יִצְחָ֣ק בְּנ֑וֹ וַיְבַקַּע֙ עֲצֵ֣י עֹלָ֔ה וַיָּ֣קׇם וַיֵּ֔לֶךְ אֶל־הַמָּק֖וֹם אֲשֶׁר־אָֽמַר־ל֥וֹ הָאֱלֹהִֽים׃
verses.douay_rheims: So Abraham rising up in the night, saddled his ass, and took with him two young men, and Isaac his son: and when he had cut wood for the holocaust, he went his way to the place which God had commanded him.
verses.finnish_biblia: Niin Abraham nousi varhain aamulla, ja valjasti aasinsa, ja otti kaksi palveliaansa kanssansa, ja poikansa Isaakin; ja halkoili puita polttouhriin, ja valmisti itsensä, ja meni sille paikalle, minkä Jumala oli hänelle sanonut.
verses.king_james: And Abraham rose up early in the morning, and saddled his ass, and took two of his young men with him, and Isaac his son, and clave the wood for the burnt offering, and rose up, and went unto the place of which God had told him.

verses.latin_vulgate: Die autem tertio, elevatis oculis, vidit locum procul :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: בַּיּ֣וֹם הַשְּׁלִישִׁ֗י וַיִּשָּׂ֨א אַבְרָהָ֧ם אֶת־עֵינָ֛יו וַיַּ֥רְא אֶת־הַמָּק֖וֹם מֵרָחֹֽק׃
verses.douay_rheims: And on the third day, lifting up his eyes, he saw the place afar off.
verses.finnish_biblia: Kolmantena päivänä nosti Abraham silmänsä, ja näki sen paikan taampana.
verses.king_james: Then on the third day Abraham lifted up his eyes, and saw the place afar off.

verses.latin_vulgate: dixitque ad pueros suos : Expectate hic cum asino : ego et puer illuc usque properantes, postquam adoraverimus, revertemur ad vos.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֹּ֨אמֶר אַבְרָהָ֜ם אֶל־נְעָרָ֗יו שְׁבוּ־לָכֶ֥ם פֹּה֙ עִֽם־הַחֲמ֔וֹר וַאֲנִ֣י וְהַנַּ֔עַר נֵלְכָ֖ה עַד־כֹּ֑ה וְנִֽשְׁתַּחֲוֶ֖ה וְנָשׁ֥וּבָה אֲלֵיכֶֽם׃
verses.douay_rheims: And he said to his young men: Stay you here with the ass; I and the boy will go with speed as far as yonder, and after we have worshipped, will return to you.
verses.finnish_biblia: Silloin Abraham sanoi palvelioillensa: olkaat te tässä itseksenne aasin tykönä; mutta minä ja poika käymme tuonne. Ja koska me olemme rukoilleet, niin me palajamme teidän tykönne.
verses.king_james: And Abraham said unto his young men, Abide ye here with the ass; and I and the lad will go yonder and worship, and come again to you.

verses.latin_vulgate: Tulit quoque ligna holocausti, et imposuit super Isaac filium suum : ipse vero portabat in manibus ignem et gladium. Cumque duo pergerent simul,
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיִּקַּ֨ח אַבְרָהָ֜ם אֶת־עֲצֵ֣י הָעֹלָ֗ה וַיָּ֙שֶׂם֙ עַל־יִצְחָ֣ק בְּנ֔וֹ וַיִּקַּ֣ח בְּיָד֔וֹ אֶת־הָאֵ֖שׁ וְאֶת־הַֽמַּאֲכֶ֑לֶת וַיֵּלְכ֥וּ שְׁנֵיהֶ֖ם יַחְדָּֽו׃
verses.douay_rheims: And he took the wood for the holocaust, and laid it upon Isaac his son; and he himself carried in his hands fire and a sword. And as they two went on together,
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja Abraham otti polttouhrin halvot, ja sälytti poikansa Isaakin selkään, vaan itse hän otti tulen ja veitsen käteensä; ja he kävivät molemmat ynnä.
verses.king_james: And Abraham took the wood of the burnt offering, and laid it upon Isaac his son; and he took the fire in his hand, and a knife; and they went both of them together.

verses.latin_vulgate: dixit Isaac patri suo : Pater mi. At ille respondit : Quid vis, fili ? Ecce, inquit, ignis et ligna : ubi est victima holocausti ?
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֹּ֨אמֶר יִצְחָ֜ק אֶל־אַבְרָהָ֤ם אָבִיו֙ וַיֹּ֣אמֶר אָבִ֔י וַיֹּ֖אמֶר הִנֶּ֣נִּֽי בְנִ֑י וַיֹּ֗אמֶר הִנֵּ֤ה הָאֵשׁ֙ וְהָ֣עֵצִ֔ים וְאַיֵּ֥ה הַשֶּׂ֖ה לְעֹלָֽה׃
verses.douay_rheims: Isaac said to his father: My father. And he answered: What wilt thou, son? Behold, saith he, fire and wood: where is the victim for the holocaust?
verses.finnish_biblia: Niin sanoi Isaak isällensä Abrahamille: minun isäni. Hän vastasi: katso, tässä minä olen, poikani. Ja hän sanoi: katso, tässä on tuli ja halvot; mutta kussa on lammas polttouhriksi?
verses.king_james: And Isaac spake unto Abraham his father, and said, My father: and he said, Here am I, my son. And he said, Behold the fire and the wood: but where is the lamb for a burnt offering?

verses.latin_vulgate: Dixit autem Abraham : Deus providebit sibi victimam holocausti, fili mi. Pergebant ergo pariter.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֹּ֙אמֶר֙ אַבְרָהָ֔ם אֱלֹהִ֞ים יִרְאֶה־לּ֥וֹ הַשֶּׂ֛ה לְעֹלָ֖ה בְּנִ֑י וַיֵּלְכ֥וּ שְׁנֵיהֶ֖ם יַחְדָּֽו׃
verses.douay_rheims: And Abraham said: God will provide himself a victim for an holocaust, my son. So they went on together.
verses.finnish_biblia: Abraham vastasi: Jumala on edeskatsova itsellensä lampaan polttouhriksi, poikani. Ja he kävivät molemmat ynnä.
verses.king_james: And Abraham said, My son, God will provide himself a lamb for a burnt offering: so they went both of them together.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et venerunt ad locum quem ostenderat ei Deus, in quo ædificavit altare, et desuper ligna composuit ; cumque alligasset Isaac filium suum, posuit eum in altare super struem lignorum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיָּבֹ֗אוּ אֶֽל־הַמָּקוֹם֮ אֲשֶׁ֣ר אָֽמַר־ל֣וֹ הָאֱלֹהִים֒ וַיִּ֨בֶן שָׁ֤ם אַבְרָהָם֙ אֶת־הַמִּזְבֵּ֔חַ וַֽיַּעֲרֹ֖ךְ אֶת־הָעֵצִ֑ים וַֽיַּעֲקֹד֙ אֶת־יִצְחָ֣ק בְּנ֔וֹ וַיָּ֤שֶׂם אֹתוֹ֙ עַל־הַמִּזְבֵּ֔חַ מִמַּ֖עַל לָעֵצִֽים׃
verses.douay_rheims: And they came to the place which God had shewn him, where he built an altar, and laid the wood in order upon it; and when he had bound Isaac his son, he laid him on the altar upon the pile of wood.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja kuin he tulivat sille paikalle, josta Jumala oli hänelle sanonut, teki Abraham siihen alttarin, ja pani halvot; ja sitoi poikansa Isaakin, ja pani hänen alttarille halkoin päälle.
verses.king_james: And they came to the place which God had told him of; and Abraham built an altar there, and laid the wood in order, and bound Isaac his son, and laid him on the altar upon the wood.

verses.latin_vulgate: Extenditque manum, et arripuit gladium, ut immolaret filium suum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיִּשְׁלַ֤ח אַבְרָהָם֙ אֶת־יָד֔וֹ וַיִּקַּ֖ח אֶת־הַֽמַּאֲכֶ֑לֶת לִשְׁחֹ֖ט אֶת־בְּנֽוֹ׃
verses.douay_rheims: And he put forth his hand, and took the sword, to sacrifice his son.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja ojensi kätensä, ja sivalsi veitsen, teurastaaksensa poikaansa.
verses.king_james: And Abraham stretched forth his hand, and took the knife to slay his son.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et ecce angelus Domini de cælo clamavit, dicens : Abraham, Abraham. Qui respondit : Adsum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיִּקְרָ֨א אֵלָ֜יו מַלְאַ֤ךְ יְהֹוָה֙ מִן־הַשָּׁמַ֔יִם וַיֹּ֖אמֶר אַבְרָהָ֣ם ׀ אַבְרָהָ֑ם וַיֹּ֖אמֶר הִנֵּֽנִי׃
verses.douay_rheims: And behold, an angel of the Lord from heaven called to him, saying: Abraham, Abraham. And he answered: Here I am.
verses.finnish_biblia: Niin Herran enkeli huusi häntä taivaasta, sanoen: Abraham, Abraham. Hän vastasi: tässä minä olen.
verses.king_james: And the angel of the Lord called unto him out of heaven, and said, Abraham, Abraham: and he said, Here am I.

verses.latin_vulgate: Dixitque ei : Non extendas manum tuam super puerum, neque facias illi quidquam : nunc cognovi quod times Deum, et non pepercisti unigenito filio tuo propter me.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֹּ֗אמֶר אַל־תִּשְׁלַ֤ח יָֽדְךָ֙ אֶל־הַנַּ֔עַר וְאַל־תַּ֥עַשׂ ל֖וֹ מְא֑וּמָה כִּ֣י ׀ עַתָּ֣ה יָדַ֗עְתִּי כִּֽי־יְרֵ֤א אֱלֹהִים֙ אַ֔תָּה וְלֹ֥א חָשַׂ֛כְתָּ אֶת־בִּנְךָ֥ אֶת־יְחִידְךָ֖ מִמֶּֽנִּי׃
verses.douay_rheims: And he said to him: Lay not thy hand upon the boy, neither do thou any thing to him: now I know that thou fearest God, and hast not spared thy only begotten son for my sake.
verses.finnish_biblia: Hän sanoi: älä satuta kättäs poikaan, älä myös hänelle mitään tee: sillä nyt minä tiedän, että sinä pelkäät Jumalaa, ja et ole säästänyt ainokaista poikaas minun tähteni.
verses.king_james: And he said, Lay not thine hand upon the lad, neither do thou any thing unto him: for now I know that thou fearest God, seeing thou hast not withheld thy son, thine only son from me.

verses.latin_vulgate: Levavit Abraham oculos suos, viditque post tergum arietem inter vepres hærentem cornibus, quem assumens obtulit holocaustum pro filio.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיִּשָּׂ֨א אַבְרָהָ֜ם אֶת־עֵינָ֗יו וַיַּרְא֙ וְהִנֵּה־אַ֔יִל אַחַ֕ר נֶאֱחַ֥ז בַּסְּבַ֖ךְ בְּקַרְנָ֑יו וַיֵּ֤לֶךְ אַבְרָהָם֙ וַיִּקַּ֣ח אֶת־הָאַ֔יִל וַיַּעֲלֵ֥הוּ לְעֹלָ֖ה תַּ֥חַת בְּנֽוֹ׃
verses.douay_rheims: Abraham lifted up his eyes, and saw behind his back a ram, amongst the briers, sticking fast by the horns, which he took and offered for a holocaust instead of his son.
verses.finnish_biblia: Niin Abraham nosti silmänsä, ja äkkäsi oinaan takanansa, sarvista sekaantuneena tihkiään pensastoon. Niin Abraham meni, ja otti oinaan ja uhrasi polttouhriksi poikansa edestä.
verses.king_james: And Abraham lifted up his eyes, and looked, and behold behind him a ram caught in a thicket by his horns: and Abraham went and took the ram, and offered him up for a burnt offering in the stead of his son.

verses.latin_vulgate: Appellavitque nomen loci illius, Dominus videt. Unde usque hodie dicitur : In monte Dominus videbit.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיִּקְרָ֧א אַבְרָהָ֛ם שֵֽׁם־הַמָּק֥וֹם הַה֖וּא יְהֹוָ֣ה ׀ יִרְאֶ֑ה אֲשֶׁר֙ יֵאָמֵ֣ר הַיּ֔וֹם בְּהַ֥ר יְהֹוָ֖ה יֵרָאֶֽה׃
verses.douay_rheims: And he called the name of that place, The Lord seeth. Whereupon, even to this day, it is said: In the mountain the Lord will see.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja Abraham nimitti sen paikan, Herra on edeskatsova: josta vielä tänäpänä sanotaan, Herran vuorella edeskatsotaan.
verses.king_james: And Abraham called the name of that place Jehovah–jireh: as it is said to this day, In the mount of the Lord it shall be seen.

verses.latin_vulgate: Vocavit autem angelus Domini Abraham secundo de cælo, dicens :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיִּקְרָ֛א מַלְאַ֥ךְ יְהֹוָ֖ה אֶל־אַבְרָהָ֑ם שֵׁנִ֖ית מִן־הַשָּׁמָֽיִם׃
verses.douay_rheims: And the angel of the Lord called to Abraham a second time from heaven, saying:
verses.finnish_biblia: Mutta Herran enkeli huusi Abrahamia toistamiseen taivaasta.
verses.king_james: And the angel of the Lord called unto Abraham out of heaven the second time,

verses.latin_vulgate: Per memetipsum juravi, dicit Dominus : quia fecisti hanc rem, et non pepercisti filio tuo unigenito propter me :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֹּ֕אמֶר בִּ֥י נִשְׁבַּ֖עְתִּי נְאֻם־יְהֹוָ֑ה כִּ֗י יַ֚עַן אֲשֶׁ֤ר עָשִׂ֙יתָ֙ אֶת־הַדָּבָ֣ר הַזֶּ֔ה וְלֹ֥א חָשַׂ֖כְתָּ אֶת־בִּנְךָ֥ אֶת־יְחִידֶֽךָ׃
verses.douay_rheims: By my own self have I sworn, saith the Lord: because thou hast done this thing, and hast not spared thy only begotten son for my sake:
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja sanoi: Minä olen vannonut itse kauttani, sanoo Herra: ettäs tämän teit, ja et säästänyt sinun ainoaa poikaas;
verses.king_james: And said, By myself have I sworn, saith the Lord, for because thou hast done this thing, and hast not withheld thy son, thine only son:

verses.latin_vulgate: benedicam tibi, et multiplicabo semen tuum sicut stellas cæli, et velut arenam quæ est in littore maris : possidebit semen tuum portas inimicorum suorum,
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כִּֽי־בָרֵ֣ךְ אֲבָרֶכְךָ֗ וְהַרְבָּ֨ה אַרְבֶּ֤ה אֶֽת־זַרְעֲךָ֙ כְּכוֹכְבֵ֣י הַשָּׁמַ֔יִם וְכַח֕וֹל אֲשֶׁ֖ר עַל־שְׂפַ֣ת הַיָּ֑ם וְיִרַ֣שׁ זַרְעֲךָ֔ אֵ֖ת שַׁ֥עַר אֹיְבָֽיו׃
verses.douay_rheims: I will bless thee, and I will multiply thy seed as the stars of heaven, and as the sand that is by the sea shore; thy seed shall possess the gates of their enemies.
verses.finnish_biblia: Niin minä suuresti siunaan sinun, ja runsaasti lisään sinun siemenes niinkuin taivaan tähdet, ja niinkuin sannan meren reunalla. Ja sinun siemenes on perivä vihollistensa portit.
verses.king_james: That in blessing I will bless thee, and in multiplying I will multiply thy seed as the stars of the heaven, and as the sand which is upon the sea shore; and thy seed shall possess the gate of his enemies;

verses.latin_vulgate: et benedicentur in semine tuo omnes gentes terræ, quia obedisti voci meæ.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְהִתְבָּרְכ֣וּ בְזַרְעֲךָ֔ כֹּ֖ל גּוֹיֵ֣י הָאָ֑רֶץ עֵ֕קֶב אֲשֶׁ֥ר שָׁמַ֖עְתָּ בְּקֹלִֽי׃
verses.douay_rheims: And in thy seed shall all the nations of the earth be blessed, because thou hast obeyed my voice.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja sinun siemenessäs pitää kaikki kansat maan päällä siunatuksi tuleman, ettäs minun äänelleni kuuliainen olit.
verses.king_james: And in thy seed shall all the nations of the earth be blessed; because thou hast obeyed my voice.

verses.latin_vulgate: Reversus est Abraham ad pueros suos, abieruntque Bersabee simul, et habitavit ibi.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיָּ֤שׇׁב אַבְרָהָם֙ אֶל־נְעָרָ֔יו וַיָּקֻ֛מוּ וַיֵּלְכ֥וּ יַחְדָּ֖ו אֶל־בְּאֵ֣ר שָׁ֑בַע וַיֵּ֥שֶׁב אַבְרָהָ֖ם בִּבְאֵ֥ר שָֽׁבַע׃ {פ}
verses.douay_rheims: Abraham returned to his young men, and they went to Bersabee together, and he dwelt there.
verses.finnish_biblia: Sitte palasi Abraham palveliainsa tykö, ja he nousivat, ja ynnä matkustivat BerSabaan. Ja Abraham asui BerSabassa.
verses.king_james: So Abraham returned unto his young men, and they rose up and went together to Beer–sheba; and Abraham dwelt at Beer–sheba.

verses.latin_vulgate: His ita gestis, nuntiatum est Abrahæ quod Melcha quoque genuisset filios Nachor fratri suo :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיְהִ֗י אַחֲרֵי֙ הַדְּבָרִ֣ים הָאֵ֔לֶּה וַיֻּגַּ֥ד לְאַבְרָהָ֖ם לֵאמֹ֑ר הִ֠נֵּ֠ה יָלְדָ֨ה מִלְכָּ֥ה גַם־הִ֛וא בָּנִ֖ים לְנָח֥וֹר אָחִֽיךָ׃
verses.douay_rheims: After these things, it was told Abraham, that Melcha also had borne children to Nachor his brother.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja koska nämät olivat tapahtuneet, ilmoitettiin Abrahamille sanoen: katso, Milka on myös synnyttänyt poikia sinun veljelles Nahorille.
verses.king_james: And it came to pass after these things, that it was told Abraham, saying, Behold, Milcah, she hath also born children unto thy brother Nahor;

verses.latin_vulgate: Hus primogenitum, et Buz fratrem ejus, et Camuel patrem Syrorum,
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אֶת־ע֥וּץ בְּכֹר֖וֹ וְאֶת־בּ֣וּז אָחִ֑יו וְאֶת־קְמוּאֵ֖ל אֲבִ֥י אֲרָֽם׃
verses.douay_rheims: Hus, the firstborn, and Buz, his brother, and Camuel the father of the Syrians,
verses.finnish_biblia: Esikoisensa Utsin, ja Butsin hänen veljensä, ja Kemuelin Aramin isän.
verses.king_james: Huz his firstborn, and Buz his brother, and Kemuel the father of Aram,

verses.latin_vulgate: et Cased, et Azau, Pheldas quoque et Jedlaph,
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְאֶת־כֶּ֣שֶׂד וְאֶת־חֲז֔וֹ וְאֶת־פִּלְדָּ֖שׁ וְאֶת־יִדְלָ֑ף וְאֵ֖ת בְּתוּאֵֽל׃
verses.douay_rheims: And Cased, and Azau, and Pheldas, and Jedlaph,
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja Kesedin ja Hasonin, ja Pildaksen ja Jedlaphen, ja Betuelin.
verses.king_james: And Chesed, and Hazo, and Pildash, and Jidlaph, and Bethuel.

verses.latin_vulgate: ac Bathuel, de quo nata est Rebecca : octo istos genuit Melcha, Nachor fratri Abrahæ.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וּבְתוּאֵ֖ל יָלַ֣ד אֶת־רִבְקָ֑ה שְׁמֹנָ֥ה אֵ֙לֶּה֙ יָלְדָ֣ה מִלְכָּ֔ה לְנָח֖וֹר אֲחִ֥י אַבְרָהָֽם׃
verses.douay_rheims: And Bathuel, of whom was born Rebecca: these eight did Melcha bear to Nachor, Abraham's brother.
verses.finnish_biblia: Mutta Betuel siitti Rebekan. Nämät kahdeksan synnytti Milka Nahorille Abrahamin veljelle.
verses.king_james: And Bethuel begat Rebekah: these eight Milcah did bear to Nahor, Abraham’s brother.

verses.latin_vulgate: Concubina vero illius, nomine Roma, peperit Tabee, et Gaham, et Thahas, et Maacha.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וּפִֽילַגְשׁ֖וֹ וּשְׁמָ֣הּ רְאוּמָ֑ה וַתֵּ֤לֶד גַּם־הִוא֙ אֶת־טֶ֣בַח וְאֶת־גַּ֔חַם וְאֶת־תַּ֖חַשׁ וְאֶֽת־מַעֲכָֽה׃ {פ}
verses.douay_rheims: And his concubine, named Roma, bore Tabee, and Gaham, and Tahas, and Maacha.
verses.finnish_biblia: Mutta hänen jalkavaimonsa, Rehuma nimeltä, synnytti Teban, Gahamin, Tehaksen ja Maakan.
verses.king_james: And his concubine, whose name was Reumah, she bare also Tebah, and Gaham, and Thahash, and Maachah.
